# TESS Program G08020

Title: Understanding The Origin And Evolutionary Context Of Variability In Alpha Cygni Variables

PI: Guzik, Joyce - Los Alamos National Laboratory

Type: SMALL

Summary: This project leverages TESS s photometry to observe 75 Alpha Cygni variables luminous B- and A-type supergiants exhibiting quasi-periodic or aperiodic low-amplitude brightness variations (0.01 0.1 mag) on timescales of days to months. Ground-based observations lack the cadence, precision, and continuity to characterize the variability and detect transient phenomena. Using TESS Cycle 8 s observations for an extended 56-day sector and subsequent overlapping sectors, we will quantify light curve properties including searching for recurrent phenomena such as those reported in the prototype, Deneb. We will use TESS data in conjunction with 1-D evolution and pulsation models to investigate causes of variability and to determine interior properties and evolutionary state of these stars.


# Sector 097 targets observed (2 min: 40, 20 s: 0)
